meaning: vt.叙述,讲,使联系,发生关系
examples: Trainees should be invited to relate new ideas to their past experiences.
I don't think he understood the dynamics of how the police and the city administration relate.
Many adults can't relate to children.
Common concerns about keeping a city up all night relate to noise, traffic and alcohol consumption.
The most instructive findings of the report relate to economic damages from extreme weather events.
